acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Related-divergence migration preserves the peer fixture's later `api` label on `bd-rich-open`.
- [ ] The fix does not regress same-edge dep convergence or peer-only bead preservation.
- [ ] The realistic divergence integration test asserts the label behavior directly.
- [ ] Targeted migration tests pass for both local-only peer migration and related-divergence merge.
description:
**Problem**
While hardening the realistic fixture suite in `bd-dgsk.13`, the related-divergence migration scenario exposed a real merge regression. When local `rich_workflow` is merged with remote descendant `rich_workflow_peer`, the migrated result keeps the peer-only bead and shared dep convergence, but drops the later `api` label from `bd-rich-open` even though the peer fixture carries a later `_at` stamp and local-only migration of `rich_workflow_peer` preserves that label.

This means the migration executor's local/remote merge path is not faithfully preserving at least one later legacy label-array update under realistic divergence.

**Files**: crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs, crates/beads-git/src/wire.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store_corpus/rich_workflow*
design:
**Design**
Investigate the migration merge path for related local/remote divergence and fix the loss of later label-array state.

1. Reproduce the failing `rich_workflow` + `rich_workflow_peer` migration merge and isolate whether the loss happens in legacy state parsing, canonical join, or migrate executor ancestry/retry handling.
2. Preserve the peer-side `bd-rich-open` label set in the merged canonical state when the peer bead has the later winning write stamp.
3. Keep the existing deterministic dep/dot behavior and peer-only bead preservation intact.
4. Strengthen the realistic divergence test so the regression is pinned permanently.
title: Migration follow-up: related-divergence merge drops later legacy label-array update

closed_reason: Too much complexity for now; prefer structured logs
description:
**Problem**
We need local‑first performance visibility without relying on a centralized service. Current metrics are only in-memory snapshots + tracing events.

**Design**
- Add a local observability store (SQLite) for metrics time‑series (and optionally log metadata) under `<data_dir>/obs/obs.db`.
- Record metrics events with timestamp + labels; expose an admin query to fetch ranges.
- Keep storage bounded (retention by time or max rows).
- Optional: store log index/metadata (not full messages) for quick filtering.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Metrics time‑series are persisted locally and queryable via admin API/CLI.
- [ ] Storage is bounded via retention config.
- [ ] Works offline; no external service required.

**Files:** src/daemon/metrics.rs, src/daemon/admin.rs, src/api/admin.rs, src/cli/commands/admin.rs, src/paths.rs
title: Observability: local metrics store (SQLite)

description:
**Problem**
When fixture code or CLI setup hits IPC autostart, immediate daemon startup failures can degrade into long socket waits instead of actionable errors. The current spawn path also does a broad pre-exec file-descriptor close sweep, which is a plausible source of startup stalls under large open-file limits. That makes test failures slower and harder to trust.

**Design**
Instrument the IPC autostart path so child startup failures surface immediately in test workflows, and replace the broad pre-exec fd sweep with a bounded platform-appropriate strategy (`closefrom`, `close_range`, or enumerating actual open fds). Keep the fix production-safe but prove it with test-oriented receipts.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] IPC autostart startup failures become visible as immediate actionable errors in test workflows
- [ ] The fd-sanitization path is bounded and no longer scales with large `RLIMIT_NOFILE` ceilings
- [ ] Regression coverage or profiling receipts demonstrate the improvement

**Files**
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/client.rs`
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/spawn_sanitizer.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/**`
- `docs/PERF_HOTPATHS.md`

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Workspace: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs
jj: bc7b844925c2 (rqunwlyvusox)

Made IPC autostart failures observable and bounded without regressing launcher overrides.
- Built-in `bd daemon run` autostart now fail-fasts on direct early exit instead of degrading into long socket waits.
- Override autostart programs remain launcher-compatible: the client waits for the socket instead of assuming the wrapper process itself must stay alive until the daemon is ready.
- Default beads-managed socket dirs still get `0700`, but custom socket parents are only created, not chmodded.
- Bounded fd sanitization moved into platform-appropriate spawn code: macOS uses `posix_spawn(... POSIX_SPAWN_CLOEXEC_DEFAULT ...)`, Linux/Android use the raw `close_range` syscall, and unsupported Unix falls back to a child-side `_SC_OPEN_MAX` sweep.
- Strong review on this bead flushed three real issues in the first cut: the parent-side fd snapshot race, false fast-fail for launcher-style overrides, and Linux symbol portability. The closing tree fixes all three.

Verification:
- `cargo test -p beads-surface ipc:: -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration cli::critical_path::test_auto_init_on_first_create -- --exact --nocapture`
- `cargo check -p beads-surface --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`
- `cargo check -p beads-surface --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `just dylint`
- `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo xtest`
- `cargo nextest run --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests`

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Workspace: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs
jj: 8798d569cd6d (lzqvvrnxursq)

Follow-up after strong adversarial review.
- Restored the default socket privacy invariant in the autostart path: default-managed socket parents now re-apply `0700` during autostart, while explicit custom socket parents still avoid chmod churn.
- Added `connect_with_autostart_restricts_default_socket_parent` to prove the regression stays fixed.
- Fixed the Apple-only test cleanup path so `spawn_sanitizer` tests kill and reap spawned children instead of leaving them to exit naturally.

Verification:
- `cargo test -p beads-surface ipc:: -- --nocapture`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `just dylint`
- `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo xtest`
- `cargo nextest run --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests`
- `cargo nextest run --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests --status-level leak --final-status-level none` (clean rerun after one non-reproducing leaky report)

title: Make IPC autostart failures observable and bounded in test workflows
